entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: A polyphagous pest known for its high diversity of digestive proteases.

entities.name: POLYPHAGY
entities.description: The ability of an organism to feed on a wide variety of host plants.

entities.name: SERINE PROTEASES
entities.description: Digestive enzymes that contribute significantly to dietary protein digestion in H. armigera.

entities.name: RNA INTERFERENCE
entities.description: A biological process used to silence specific gene expression by using double-stranded RNA.

entities.name: HATRY1
entities.description: An isoform of serine protease whose expression levels are proportional to larval growth rate.

entities.name: LARVAL GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate at which larvae increase in size and mass.

entities.name: DOUBLE-STRANDED RNA
entities.description: The molecule used to trigger RNA interference for the silencing of target genes.

entities.name: LARVAL WEIGHT
entities.description: The physical mass of the larvae, which is reduced upon silencing of serine protease isoforms.

entities.name: HATRY13
entities.description: An isoform of serine protease that is over-expressed as a compensatory response when HaTry1 is silenced.

entities.name: COMPENSATORY EFFECT
entities.description: A biological response where the over-expression of one gene offsets the silencing of another.

entities.name: PHYLOGENETIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: A method used to determine the evolutionary relationship between different protease isoforms.

relationships.description: closely related according to phylogenetic analysis
